subject: Chemistry
solution: Ag(NH3)2+OH- is called Tollens' reagent and it is a mild oxidizing reagent that oxidizes aldehyde groups to their respective acid and it gets reduced. It does not affect ketone group. While NaBH4 is a reducing agent which can reduce aldehyde or ketone group in alcohols but it does not reduce carboxylic acid.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Phenyl magnesium bromide is called Grignard reagent. One mole of this react with carbonyl compound to give alcohols. It react with aldehyde to form secondary alcohol while with ketones gives tertiary alcohols. The given compound is a tertiary alcohol so, reactant should be a ketone.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Carbohydrate molecules have hemiacetal or hemiketal groups can reduces Tollen's reagent and Fehling's solution are reducing. All monosaccharides are reducing sugars. If any sugar is having free –OH group at anomeric carbon then it will be a reducing sugar.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The relation between half-life and initial concentration of nth order reaction can be written as follows,

t12∝1A0n-1

100∝10.5n-1

50∝11n-1

21=10.5n-1

21=2n-1

n-1=1

n=2

order =2

For the second order reactions half-life is inversely proportional to initial concentration.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Non-ionic detergents do not contain any ion in their constitution. One such detergent is formed when stearic acid reacts with polyethylene glycol.

CH3CH216COOHStearic acid+HOCH2CH2OnCH2CH2OHPolyethyleneglycol →-H2OCH3CH216COOCH2CH2OnCH2CH2OH

 A type of detergent in which the active part of the molecule is a positive ion (cation). Cationic detergents are usually quaternary ammonium salts.

Anionic detergent is a synthetic detergent in which a lipophilic hydrocarbon group of the molecule is an anion. A detergent molecule consists of a long hydrocarbon chain and a water-soluble negative ionic group.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.
solution:
In Cl2 molecule, the covalent radius is half of the internuclear distance, so statement I is false. The covalent radius is little smaller than the actual radius of the atom of chlorine due to overlapping during formation of covalent bond.

For the same element, anion has lower effective nuclear charge than atom so, anion is larger than the parent atom. That means statement II is correct.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Characteristics of aromatic compounds include:
- Must be Cyclic and planar
- Must have 4n+2π electrons n=1, 2, 3, 4, ....
- Resist Addition but Prefer Substitution.
- Must Possess Resonance Energy.
Due to severe non-bonded interactions between the internal hydrogen (as shown in figure), the ring assumes non-planar geometry in 10-annulene, hence, it is not an aromatic compound.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Metals
options: Less tendency to be soluble in melts than impurities
solution:
This process is used for refining easily fusible metals like lead and tin. The impure metal is heated on the sloped hearth of a reverberatory furnace. The metal melts and flows down leaving the impurities.

Liquation is a metallurgical method for separating metals from an ore or alloy. The material must be heated until one of the metals starts to melt and drain away from the other and can be collected. This method was largely used to remove lead containing silver from copper, but it can also be used to remove antimony minerals from ore, and refine tin.

Liquation is used to purify metals having lower melting point than impurities present in them. For example, Pb, Sn, Sb, Bi and Hg.

subject: Chemistry
options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mfenced></math> are true and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mfenced></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math>.

solution: Under the Friedel Craft Reaction condition, the aniline (a Lewis base) binds to the electrophile AlCl3 (a Lewis acid) to give a coordination complex (a salt). Then, the positive charge on the nitrogen is electron withdrawing, and it pulls the electron density of the aromatic ring by -I effect. It therefore, deactivates the ring for further alkylation reactions. Finally, the complex precipitates out of the reaction mixture and the alkylation reaction is not observed.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In summer season, chlorine atoms forming chlorine sinks, preventing much ozone depletion, whereas In winter, special type of clouds called polar stratospheric clouds are formed over Antarctica.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The Birch reduction is an organic reaction where aromatic compounds which have a benzenoid ring are converted into 1,4-cyclohexadiene which have two hydrogen atoms attached at opposite ends of the molecule. The solvated electrons (the free electrons in the solution of sodium in liquid ammonia which are responsible for the intense blue colour) add to the aromatic ring, giving a radical anion. This radical anion is supplied with a proton by the alcohol. The alcohol also supplies another proton to the penultimate carbanion. Now, with the alcohol present, the products 1,4– cyclohexadiene and an alkoxide ion are formed.

The reaction with nitro anisole is given below.

Na+NH3→NaNH3++ammoniated electrons

subject: Chemistry
solution: In the presence of ultraviolet light (but without a catalyst present), hot benzene will also undergo an addition reaction with chlorine. The ring delocalisation is permanently broken, and a chlorine atom adds on to each carbon atom. 1,2,3,4,5,6-hexachlorocyclohexane or benzenehexachloride is formed as a product. It is an example of free radical addition reaction.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
H2O2 decomposes slowly on exposure to light.
2H2O2l→2H2Ol+O2g
In the presence of metal surfaces or traces of alkali (present in glass containers), the above reaction is catalysed. It is, therefore, stored in wax-lined glass or plastic vessels in dark. Urea can be added as a stabiliser. It is kept away from dust because dust can induce explosive decomposition of the compound. Urea act as negative catalysts in the decomposition of hydrogen peroxide.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Reaction of ammonia with diborane gives initially B2H6.2NH3 which is formulated as BH2NH32+ BH4- ; further heating gives borazine, B3N3H6 known as ''inorganic benzene'' in view of its ring structure with alternate BH and NH groups.

3B2H6+6NH3→3BH2NH32+BH4-→Heat2B3N3H6+12H2

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Disproportionation reaction, is a type of redox reaction involving simultaneous reduction and oxidation of atoms of the same element from one oxidation state (OS) to two different oxidation states.

2H2O2-1⟶2H2O-2+O20 : Disproportionation

2NO2+H2O→HNO3+HNO2 : Disproportionation

MnO4-+4H++3e-→MnO2+2H2O : reduction only

3MnO42-+4H+→2MnO4-+MnO2+4+2H2O : Disproportionation

All the alkali earth metals, except beryllium combine with hydrogen upon heating to form their hydrides, MH2. BeH2, however, can be prepared by the reaction of BeCl2 with LiAlH4. .

2BeCl2+LiAlH4→2BeH2+LiCl+AlCl3

solution:
~p⇔~q∧q  =p⇔q∧q

Proof: Solving the given ~p⇔~q

=~p→~q∧~q→p

=~~p∨~q∧q∨p

=~~p∧q∧p∨q

=p∧q∨~p∨q      ...1

Now, p⇔q≡p→q∧q→p

=~p∨q∧~q∨p

=~p∨q∧~q∨~p∨q∧p

=~p∧~q∨q∧~q∨~p∧p∨q∧p

=~p∧~q∨c∨c∨q∧p where c is contradiction

=~p∧~q∨p∧q

=~p∨q∨p∧q        ...2

So from equation 1 & 2 we get

~p⇔~q∧q≡p⇔q∧q

p q p↔q p↔q∧q p→q p→q∧q p→1∧p
T T T T T T T
T F F F F F F
F T F F T T F
F F T F T F F

∴  ~p⇔~q∧q is equivalent to p⇒q∧p.

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Let the five digit number be abcde

∵   36=22×32

Case I: When exactly one digit is 1

So, number of permutations of 2,2,3,3,1 will be

 =5!2!2!=30

Case II: When exactly two digits are 1

So, number of permutations of 4,3,3,1,1,6,2,3,1,1 or 9,2,2,1,1 will be

=5!2!2!+5!2!+5!2!2!=120

Case III: When exactly three digits are 1

So, number of permutations of 4,9,1,1,1 or 6,6,1,1,1 will be

=5!3!+5!3!2!=30

Hence, the required numbers=30+120+30=180

subject: Mathematics
solution:
Given, p2+q2x2-2qp+rx+q2+r2=0

On simplifying we get, px-q2+qx-r2=0

⇒px-q=0 & qx-r=0

⇒x=qp=rq

⇒  x=qp=rq=4    [because roots of equation x2-2x-8=0 are 4 or -2]

As p,q,r are positive, so x must be 4.

Now, q=4p and r=4q=16p

So, q2+r2p2=4p2+4×4p2p2=16+256=272.

solution:
Given the equations of the sides AB,BC and CA of a triangle ABC are 2x+y=0,x+py=15a and x-y=3 respectively.

Now on solving equation 2x+y=0,x+py=15a and x-y=3 we get coordinates of A1,-2,B15a1-2p,-30a1-2p and C=18p-30p+1,15p-33p+1

And let orthocentre be H2,a

Slope of AH=a+21

Sloe of BC=-1p

As AH⊥BC, so p=a+2

Now coordinate of C=18p-30p+1,15p-33p+1

So, slope of HC

=15p-33p+1-a18p-30p+1-2=15p-33-p-2p+118p-30-2p-2

=16p-p2-3116p-32

Now HC⊥AB, so slope of HC× slope of AB=-1

⇒16p-p2-3116p-32×-2=-1

⇒p2-8p+15=0

⇒p=3 or 5

But if p=5 then a=3 not acceptable as p=a+2

∴p=3

subject: Physics
solution:
At very high frequencies, inductor acts as an open circuit and capacitor as short circuit.

So, capacitive reactance, XC=1ωC≈0

Also, inductive reactance, XL=ωL≈∞

Thus, equivalent circuit can be redrawn as

Here, both 4 Ω resistances are in parallel and together they are in series with1 Ω and 2 Ω resistances.

Equivalent impedance is Z=1+4×44+4+2=5 Ω

Effective current is I=220 V5 Ω=44 A

subject: Physics
solution:
Using Rydberg formula,

1λ=R1nf2-1ni2, where, R is Rydberg constant and n is quantum number.

For first line of Lyman series

1λ=R1-14=R34

⇒λ=43R      ...1

For 3rd  line of Paschen series

1λ3=R132-162=R9×34

For 2nd line of Balmer series

1λ2=R122-142=R4×34

Thus, aλ=λ3-λ2=12R-163R=203R

Putting equation 1

a43R=203R⇒a=5

C1=A3ε012=6 Aϵ0 and C2=A4ϵ01=4Aε0

Now, net charge stored is qnet=CeqΔV=2410Aε0100=240Aε0

Thus, voltage of the conducting foil is Vfoil =60 V

solution:
Wavelength of incident beam λ=900×10-9 m

Energy of a single photon E=hcλ.

Intensity is defined as power per unit area. Therefore,

Intensity of incident beam I=EtA

If the number of photons crossing the given area per second is n, then

n=Enet Esingle pholon =IAλhc

⇒n=1001×10-4900×10-96.62×10-34×3×108=4.5×1016.

In case of L-R circuit

Z=XL2+R2 Therefore, power factor

P1=cosϕ=RZ

As XL=R ⇒Z=2R

⇒P1=R2R⇒P1=12

In case of  L-C-R circuit

Z=R2+XL-XC2

According to the question XL=XC ⇒Z=R  ⇒P2=cosϕ=RR=1

⇒P1P2=12

subject: Physics
solution:
The ratio of electric field amplitude to the magnetic field amplitude of an electromagnetic wave is equal to the speed of light in the medium. Therefore,

c=E0B0⇒E0=cB0

⇒E0=3×108×2×10-8

⇒E0=6 V m-1

According to the given equation B→=2×10-8sin0.5×103x+1.5×1011tj^ T the wave travels in x direction. As electric field and magnetic field vibrate perpendicular to the velocity of light, electric field can not be along x axis.

g'=g1-2hR

Now, change in g is

Percentage change is

subject: Physics
solution:
Let acceleration of $100 \mathrm{~kg}$ block $=a_1$
FBD of $100 \mathrm{~kg}$ block w.r.t ground

$\mathrm{F}-\mathrm{T}-\mathrm{N}_1=100 \mathrm{a}_1$ ... (i)
FBD of 20 block wrt $100 \mathrm{~kg}$

$\mathrm{T}-20 \mathrm{~g}=20(2)$
$\mathrm{T}=240$ ... (ii)
$\mathrm{~N}_1=20 \mathrm{a}_1$ ... (iii)
FBD of $10 \mathrm{~kg}$ block wrt $100 \mathrm{~kg}$

$10 \mathrm{a}_1-240=10(2)$
$\mathrm{a}_1=26 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$
$\mathrm{~F}-240-20(26)=100 \times 26$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{F}=3360 \mathrm{~N}$

subject: Physics
options: Rope will break while climbing upward
Rope will break while going downward
solution:
Let a1 be the acceleration of monkey while moving down.

F.B.D of monkey while moving downward

Using Newton's second law of motion,

mg-T=ma1

∴  500-T=50×4⇒T=300 N

Let a2 be the acceleration of monkey while moving up.

F.B.D of monkey while moving up

Using Newton's second law of motion

T-mg=ma2

⇒  T-500=50×5

⇒T=750 N

Breaking strength of rope =750-300=350 N

∴  Rope will break while monkey is moving upward.

subject: Physics
solution:
The magnetic force on a moving charged particle is given by F→=qv→×B→. Therefore, we know that the magnetic force is perpendicular to the magnetic field. Therefore, the resulting acceleration will also be perpendicular to the magnetic field. As the scalar product of two mutually perpendicular vectors is zero,

⇒a→·B→=0 ⇒αi^-4j^·2i^+3j^=0

⇒2α-12=0⇒α=6
